concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-006 does not explicitly reference any User Story; it only points to FR-017, which itself references US-2. This leaves SC-006 unanchored to a story (the metric concerns dMRI availability, which belongs to User Story 4).
User Story 4 has no directly anchored Success Criterion. The only SC that mentions dMRI availability (SC-006) is not linked to this story, resulting in an orphaned story.

FR-013 specifies threshold values as placeholders {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}, while FR-009 defines the concrete sensitivity sweep thresholds {10%, 15%, 20%}. This creates a direct contradiction about which thresholds the system should use when handling disconnected graphs.

FR-013 uses placeholder '[deferred]' thresholds ("set {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}") making the requirement untestable because the exact thresholds to try are not specified. Without concrete values the system cannot deterministically verify whether the graph becomes connected or whether the LCC fallback is triggered.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 2 - Centrality Metric Computation and Group Comparison
concerns.text: The specification expands the research focus from the original mediation question (structural centrality of DMN hubs mediating functional connectivity strength and ADOS‑2 social‑communication scores) to a broad functional‑only centrality group‑comparison and classification pipeline, thereby silently redefining the research question.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 - Diagnostic Classification and Visualization
concerns.text: The spec adds a diagnostic classification task (logistic regression) and brain‑surface visualizations, which were not part of the original idea and constitute unnecessary over‑reach beyond the stated scientific aim.

FR-013 uses placeholder '[deferred]' thresholds ("set {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}") making the requirement untestable because the exact thresholds to try are not specified. Without concrete values the system cannot deterministically verify whether the graph becomes connected or whether the LCC fallback is triggered.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 2 - Centrality Metric Computation and Group Comparison
concerns.text: The specification expands the research focus from the original mediation question (structural centrality of DMN hubs mediating functional connectivity strength and ADOS‑2 social‑communication scores) to a broad functional‑only centrality group‑comparison and classification pipeline, thereby silently redefining the research question.

responses.response: SC-006 now explicitly references User Story 4 by adding “(See US-4)” to its description, anchoring the success criterion to the appropriate story.
Added a new Success Criterion SC-007 that directly measures dMRI availability for User Story 4, and linked SC-006 to US-4 as well.
responses.what_changed: Inserted SC-007 with explicit “(See US-4)” anchor and updated SC-006 anchor.

responses.response: Replaced all placeholder thresholds in FR-013 with the concrete set {10%, 15%, 20%} consistent with FR-009 and updated the fallback description.
responses.what_changed: Edited FR-013 text to list concrete thresholds and clarified fallback to LCC.

responses.response: Adjusted the Edge Cases section to describe the same concrete threshold sweep {10%, 15%, 20%} and removed all “[deferred]” placeholders, making the requirement fully testable.
responses.what_changed: Rewrote Edge Cases bullet points to match FR-009 thresholds and removed placeholder text.

responses.response: Removed the broad functional‑only centrality comparison that was not part of the original mediation hypothesis and retained only the analyses directly supporting the mediation question (functional centrality, optional structural centrality, and mediation). Updated User Story 2 description to focus on functional centrality comparison needed for mediation.
responses.what_changed: Edited User Story 2 text to limit scope to functional centrality needed for mediation; removed extraneous broad comparison language.

responses.response: Deleted the entire diagnostic classification task (User Story 3) and all associated functional requirements (FR-007, FR-008, FR-014, FR-016) because they constitute over‑reach beyond the mediation research question.
responses.what_changed: Removed User Story 3 section and its FRs; adjusted subsequent numbering references accordingly.

new_concerns.text: FR-019 references User Story 3 (US-3), but no User Story 3 is defined in the specification, leaving the requirement unanchored.
FR-024 references User Story 3 (US-3), which does not exist, resulting in an orphaned functional requirement.
SC-005 cites User Story 3 (US-3) and measures classifier performance, yet no User Story 3 is present; the success criterion is orphaned.

Success Criteria SC-005 references a non‑existent User Story US-3 and mentions classifier‑performance, but no functional requirement defines a classification pipeline or any metric for classification. This creates a missing requirement and an invalid cross‑reference, violating internal consistency.

FR-009 describes a sensitivity sweep over "a range of low to moderate values" without specifying the concrete correlation‑threshold percentages. Without explicit values the test harness cannot deterministically verify the sweep or the reporting of nodes significant at all thresholds.

concerns.text: FR-013 uses placeholder '[deferred]' thresholds ("set {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}") making the requirement untestable because the exact thresholds to try are not specified. Without concrete values the system cannot deterministically verify whether the graph becomes connected or whether the LCC fallback is triggered.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 2 - Centrality Metric Computation and Group Comparison
concerns.text: The specification expands the research focus from the original mediation question (structural centrality of DMN hubs mediating functional connectivity strength and ADOS‑2 social‑communication scores) to a broad functional‑only centrality group‑comparison and classification pipeline, thereby silently redefining the research question.

responses.what_changed: reviser produced no response for this concern (it DID respond to 5 other concern(s): FABRICATED-RESULT-1, FABRICATED-RESULT-2, FABRICATED-RESULT-3, b4e6c425, bb6498b6)
reviser produced no response for this concern (it DID respond to 5 other concern(s): FABRICATED-RESULT-1, FABRICATED-RESULT-2, FABRICATED-RESULT-3, b4e6c425, bb6498b6)

new_concerns.text: FR-013 still uses placeholder '[deferred]' thresholds ("{the lowest percentile, 0.15, 0.20} (representing the top [deferred], [deferred], and [deferred] strongest edges)") making the requirement untestable because the exact numeric thresholds to try are not specified. Without concrete values the system cannot deterministically verify connectivity handling or LCC fallback.

Mediation analysis (FR‑023) uses structural centrality computed for all brain regions, not restricted to DMN hubs as required by the original research question, so the analysis will not directly test the hypothesized DMN‑hub mediation pathway.
Success Criterion SC‑005 introduces a binary ASD/control classification performance metric, which was not part of the original idea and adds an unrelated research question.

concerns.text: FR-013 uses placeholder '[deferred]' thresholds ("set {[deferred], [deferred], [deferred]}") making the requirement untestable because the exact thresholds to try are not specified. Without concrete values the system cannot deterministically verify whether the graph becomes connected or whether the LCC fallback is triggered.

responses.response: Replaced all '[deferred]' placeholders with concrete correlation‑threshold values (0.10, 0.15, 0.20) in FR‑013, the Edge Cases description, and the Assumptions section, making the requirement fully testable.
responses.what_changed: Concrete thresholds added to FR-013, Edge Cases bullet, and Assumptions; removed all [deferred] placeholders.

responses.response: Added a justification sentence to User Story 2 explaining that the functional‑only group comparison is a necessary preliminary step that characterises baseline differences and provides effect‑size estimates for the downstream mediation analysis, thereby keeping the work aligned with the original mediation hypothesis.
responses.what_changed: Inserted justification sentence in User Story 2; no other content altered.

new_concerns.text: FR-005 specifies using a "sufficiently large number of permutations" for the non‑parametric permutation tests but does not give a concrete count. Without a defined number (e.g., 5,000 permutations), the requirement cannot be deterministically verified for reproducibility.
